segments.text: Elisha the prophet called one of the sons of the prophets and told him,
Put your cloak into your belt, take this flask of olive oil, and go to Ramoth-gilead.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Once you get there, look for Jehu, son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi. Go in, get him away from his companions, and take him to an inner room. Get him to leave his friends, lead him to a private room,
segments.speaker: Elisha the prophet

segments.text: take the flask of olive oil, and pour it on his head. Tell him, ‘This is what the Lord says: I anoint you king of Israel.’ Then open the door and run away! Don't wait around!
segments.speaker: Elisha the prophet

segments.text: So the young prophet went to Ramoth-gilead.
When he arrived, he saw the army commanders sitting around.
I've got a message for you, commander,
segments.speaker: Young man

segments.text: he said.
For which one of us?
segments.speaker: Young man

segments.text: Jehu asked.
For you, commander,
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: he replied.
Jehu got up and went inside, where the young prophet poured the olive oil on his head and announced to him,
This is what the Lord, the God of Israel, says: ‘I anoint you king over the Lord's people Israel.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You are going to destroy the family of Ahab, your master. You will avenge the blood of my prophets and the blood of all the Lord's servants killed by Jezebel.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: The whole house of Ahab will be eradicated—I will destroy all the males of Ahab's family in Israel, both slave and free.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: I will make the house of Ahab like the house of Jeroboam, son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha, son of Ahijah.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Dogs will eat Jezebel, Ahab's wife, at the plot of ground in Jezreel, and no one will bury her.’
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Then the young prophet opened the door and ran away.
When Jehu went back out to the other officers of his master, they asked him,
Is everything fine? Why did this crazy man come to you?
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: You know what he's like, and how he goes on and on,
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: he replied.
Liar!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said.
Please tell us what's going on.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Well, he talked to me about this and that, and he told me, ‘This is what the Lord says: I anoint you king over Israel.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They quickly grabbed their cloaks and spread them on the bare steps. They blew the trumpet and shouted,
Jehu is king!
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: So Jehu, son of Jehoshaphat, son of Nimshi, plotted rebellion against Joram. Joram and the whole Israelite army had been defending Ramoth-gilead against Hazael, king of Aram.
But Joram had returned to Jezreel to recover from the wounds he'd received fighting against Hazael, king of Aram. So Jehu said,
If you commanders want to make me king, don't let anyone leave town and go and announce it in Jezreel.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Jehu got on his chariot and went to Jezreel, for Joram was recuperating there. Ahaziah, king of Judah, was there too because he had come to visit Joram.
The watchman on the tower in Jezreel saw Jehu's soldiers approaching, and he shouted out,
I see soldiers coming!
segments.speaker: Watchman

segments.text: Choose a horseman,
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: Joram ordered.
Send him out to meet them and ask, ‘Do you come in peace?’
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: So a horseman rode off to meet Jehu, and said,
This is what the king asks: ‘Do you come in peace?’
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: What has peace got to do with you?
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Jehu replied.
Turn around and follow me.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: The watchman reported,
The messenger has reached them, but he's not returning.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: The king sent out a second horseman. He went up to them and said,
This is what the king asks: ‘Do you come in peace?’
segments.speaker: King

segments.text: What has peace got to do with you?
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Jehu replied.
Turn around and follow me.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: The watchman reported,
The messenger has reached them, but he's not returning. The driving looks like the driving of Jehu, son of Nimshi—he's a crazy driver!
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Prepare my chariot!
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: Joram shouted, and they had his chariot ready. Then Joram, king of Israel, and Ahaziah, king of Judah, set off in their separate chariots, and met Jehu on the plot of ground that was previously owned by Naboth from Jezreel.
When Joram saw Jehu, he asked him,
Do you come in peace, Jehu?
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: What peace can there be with so much prostitution and witchcraft caused by your mother Jezebel?
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Jehu replied.
Joram turned around and raced away, shouting out to Ahaziah,
It's treason, Ahaziah!
segments.speaker: Joram

segments.text: Jehu picked up his bow and shot Joram between the shoulders. The arrow went through his heart and he collapsed dead in his chariot.
Jehu said to Bidkar his officer,
Pick him up and throw him into the field of Naboth from Jezreel. Remember when you and I were riding together behind his father Ahab how the Lord made this prophecy against him:
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: ‘In the same way as I saw the blood of Naboth and the blood of his sons yesterday, says the Lord, so I will certainly repay you on this same plot of ground, says the Lord.’ Now then, following what the Lord has said, pick him up and throw him on the plot of ground.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: When Ahaziah, king of Judah, saw what happened, he raced up the road toward Beth-haggan. But Jehu chased after him, shouting,
Shoot him too!
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: So they shot Ahaziah in his chariot on the way up to Gur, near Ibleam. He managed to escape to Megiddo but he died there.
His servants took him by chariot to Jerusalem and buried him with his forefathers in his tomb in the City of David.
Ahaziah became king of Judah in the eleventh year of the reign of Joram, son of Ahab.
When Jezebel heard that Jehu had arrived in Jezreel, she put on her black eye shadow, placed jewelry in her hair, and watched from a window.
As Jehu came in through the gate, she shouted down,
Do you come in peace? Or are you like Zimri, a murderer of your master?
segments.speaker: Jezebel

segments.text: Jehu looked up at the window and shouted out,
Who is on my side? Anyone?
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Two or three eunuchs looked down at him.
Throw her down!
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: he shouted. And they threw her down. Her blood spattered on the wall and on the horses which then trampled her.
Jehu went inside and ate and drank. Then he said,
Please deal with that cursed woman and bury her, for she was a king's daughter.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: They went out to bury her but all they found were her skull, her feet, and her hands.
They went back and told Jehu, who said,
This is what the Lord said through his servant Elijah the Tishbite: ‘Dogs will eat Jezebel's flesh on the plot of ground at Jezreel.
segments.speaker: Jehu

segments.text: Jezebel's body will lie like manure in the field on the plot of ground at Jezreel, so that no one can say: This is where Jezebel is buried.’
segments.speaker: They went back and
